test_name: August 2024 US Form B SAT Math Module 1
passage: The function $g$ is defined by $g(x) = 2x - 148$. For what value of $x$ does $g(x) = 76$?

test_name: August 2024 US Form B SAT Math Module 1
passage: A district school board in a certain state is proposing a change to the time school starts for all high schools in the district. A sample of 353 high school students was selected at random from all high school students in the district. The selected students were asked whether they approved of the proposed change, and 300 students responded that they did not approve. Which of the following is the largest population to which the results of the survey can be generalized?

choices.label: A
choices.text: All high school students in the district

choices.label: C
choices.text: The 300 students who responded that they did not approve of the proposed change

choices.label: D
choices.text: All high school students in the state

test_name: August 2024 US Form B SAT Math Module 1
passage: The Mars Perseverance rover is a robot designed to collect samples of rock and soil. During an experiment, the rover moves at a constant speed on a straight path toward a designated target. The equation $ y = 390 - 142x $ gives the rover's remaining distance from its target $ y $, in meters, $ x $ hours after the start of the experiment. What is the best interpretation of 390 in this context?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The number of hours it takes the rover to reach its target

choices.label: B
choices.text: The rover's speed, in meters per hour, during the experiment

choices.label: C
choices.text: The rover's distance, in meters, from its target at the start of the experiment

choices.label: D
choices.text: The total distance, in meters, the rover travels in 1 hour

test_name: August 2024 US Form B SAT Math Module 1
passage: The graph of a polynomial function $ y = f(x) $ in the $ xy $-plane passes through the point (8,9). What is the value of $ f(8) $?

skill: Inference from sample statistics and margin of error
test_name: August 2024 US Form B SAT Math Module 1
passage: A company that produces only one type of item wants to estimate the percent of the items produced in a typical week that are defective. A random sample of 400 of the items produced in a certain week were tested. Based on the sample, it is estimated that 8.5% of all items produced by the company in this week are defective, with an associated margin of error of 2.73%. Based on the estimate and associated margin of error, which of the following is the most appropriate conclusion about all items produced by the company during this week?

choices.label: B
choices.text: It is plausible that between 5.77% and 11.23% of the items are defective.

choices.label: D
choices.text: It is plausible that more than 11.23% of the items are defective.

test_name: August 2024 US Form B SAT Math Module 1
passage: Which expression is equivalent to $\frac{20\pi^{2}}{\pi^{2}-x^{2}}-\frac{20\pi}{x+\pi}$, where $x > \pi$?

test_name: August 2024 US Form B SAT Math Module 1
passage: The equation $y = -4.9(x - 9.1)^2 + 10,700$ gives the estimated height above ground, $y$, in meters, of a plane, where $x$ is the number of seconds since it started a parabolic maneuver. If this equation is graphed in the $xy$-plane, which of the following is the best interpretation of the vertex of the graph?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The plane reached an estimated maximum height of 10,700 meters 4.9 seconds after it started the parabolic maneuver.

choices.label: B
choices.text: The plane reached an estimated maximum height of 10,700 meters 9.1 seconds after it started the parabolic maneuver.

choices.label: C
choices.text: The plane reached an estimated maximum height of 4.9 meters 10,700 seconds after it started the parabolic maneuver.

choices.label: D
choices.text: The plane reached an estimated maximum height of 9.1 meters 10,700 seconds after it started the parabolic maneuver.
